title: pericopsis angolensis f. brasseuriana
intro: Pericopsis angolensis f. brasseuriana, also known as Brasseur's African yellow-wood, is a plant species within the family Lauraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced antioxidant defenses. Brasseur's African yellow-wood is often prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and its essential oil is used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being.
